site.title: LUMINA
site.description: A curated gallery of cinematic photography exploring the intersection of light, architecture, and the human experience.

seo.metaDescription: Explore a curated collection of cinematic photography. LUMINA showcases visual narratives in light, architecture, portraiture, and landscape.
seo.keywords: photography, gallery, cinematic, art, portraits, landscapes, architecture

hero.headline:
Where Light
Becomes Story
hero.subheadline: A curated collection of cinematic imagery exploring the boundaries of visual art.

gallery.title: The Collection
gallery.subtitle: Each frame, a universe. Each shadow, a story.

images.title: Golden Horizon
images.description: The moment when the sky surrenders to the ocean, painted in gradients of fire and dusk. Shot at the Faroe Islands during the longest day of the year.
images.author: Elena Vasquez

images.title: Fractured Light
images.description: Brutalist architecture meets the afternoon sun in perfect angular geometry. The shadows carve new shapes into old concrete.
images.author: Marcus Chen

images.title: The Weight of Silence
images.description: A portrait series exploring solitude in urban environments. This frame captures a moment of profound stillness in the midst of chaos.
images.author: Sofia Andersen

images.title: Neon Cartography
images.description: Tokyo after midnight. The rain-slicked streets become mirrors for a thousand colored lights — the city as living canvas.
images.author: Kenji Watanabe

images.title: Tessellations
images.description: The mathematical beauty hidden in everyday surfaces. This series examines repetition, pattern, and the sublime in the mundane.
images.author: Elena Vasquez

images.title: Fjord Dawn
images.description: First light over the Norwegian fjords. The mist rises slowly, revealing mountains that have kept their silence for millennia.
images.author: Lars Eriksson

images.title: Glass & Steel
images.description: Reflections within reflections — a meditation on the recursive nature of modern architecture and the city's endless self-portrait.
images.author: Marcus Chen

images.title: Golden Dust
images.description: Sahara at the hour of the long shadow. The dunes reshape themselves daily, an ever-changing landscape of ancient geology.
images.author: Amara Diallo

images.title: Reverie
images.description: The space between waking and dreaming, caught in a single frame. Soft focus, deliberate haze, the suggestion of a self.
images.author: Sofia Andersen

images.title: Interference Pattern
images.description: Light refracted through water creates abstract topographies of color. Physics becomes poetry at 1/4000th of a second.
images.author: Kenji Watanabe

images.title: Grid City
images.description: Manhattan from above. The city's perfect geometry — a testament to human ambition, rendered in concrete and glass.
images.author: Elena Vasquez

images.title: The Archive
images.description: Inside the Bibliothèque nationale — the accumulated knowledge of civilization arranged in perfect symmetry. A cathedral of thought.
images.author: Marcus Chen

about.headline: The Art of Seeing
about.subheadline: We believe every frame is an act of witness.
about.story: LUMINA was founded in 2019 by a collective of photographers who shared one conviction: that photography is not the capture of light, but the capture of meaning. What began as a small studio in Copenhagen has grown into an international platform showcasing the world's most compelling visual storytellers.
about.mission: Our mission is to present photography that transcends documentation — images that ask questions, provoke feeling, and expand the boundaries of what the medium can express. We seek out work that lives at the intersection of craft and vision.
about.vision: A world where visual art is accessible to all, where a single image can bridge cultures and collapse distances. LUMINA exists to be that bridge.

team.name: Elena Vasquez
team.bio: Former Magnum Photos associate. Elena's editorial work has appeared in TIME, National Geographic, and The New Yorker.

team.name: Marcus Chen
team.bio: Architectural photographer based between Shanghai and Zurich. Known for his meditative studies of brutalist structures.

team.name: Sofia Andersen
team.bio: Copenhagen-based portrait photographer. Sofia's series 'Interior States' won the World Photography Award in 2023.

testimonials.quote: LUMINA doesn't just show photographs — it curates experiences. Every visit to the gallery feels like discovering language for emotions I didn't know I had.
testimonials.author: Isabelle Moreau

testimonials.quote: The collection here represents what photography can be at its absolute peak. Not decoration — revelation.
testimonials.author: Dr. James Okafor

testimonials.quote: Working with LUMINA changed how I see my own work. Their eye for meaning-in-form is extraordinary.
testimonials.author: Yuki Tanaka

faq.question: Can I purchase prints from the gallery?
faq.answer: Yes. All works in the collection are available as limited edition fine art prints. Each print is produced on museum-grade archival paper with pigment inks, signed and numbered by the artist. Contact us to inquire about specific works and editions.

faq.question: How do I submit my work for consideration?
faq.answer: We review portfolios on a rolling basis. Submit a curated selection of 10–20 images along with an artist statement via our contact form. We aim to respond within 6–8 weeks. Please note that we receive a high volume of submissions and can only respond to work we wish to pursue.

faq.question: Do you offer licensing for commercial use?
faq.answer: Selected works are available for commercial licensing. Usage rights vary by artist agreement. Please contact us with details of your intended use, and we'll connect you with the relevant artist or rights holder.

faq.question: Where are you located?
faq.answer: Our primary gallery is in Copenhagen, Denmark. We also maintain satellite spaces in New York and Tokyo, with rotating exhibitions throughout the year. Check our exhibitions calendar for current and upcoming shows.

faq.question: Do you offer virtual tours or online viewing rooms?
faq.answer: Yes — this website serves as our primary online viewing room. We also host periodic live virtual tours with curators and artists. Subscribe to our newsletter to be notified of upcoming events.

faq.question: What file formats are used for digital downloads?

contact.headline: Begin a Conversation
contact.subheadline: Whether you're an artist, collector, or simply curious — we'd love to hear from you.

footer.tagline: Seeing the world, one frame at a time.
